_comment: PUBLIC hybrid golden (doc CLI + --dataset). Produces standalone KB entries AND one context-overlay per table. KB entries hold ONLY cross-cutting knowledge that does not belong in any single table's overlay (dataset provenance, cross-table structure/metrics); per-table content lives in the overlays, not as duplicate KB entries. Graded on BOTH (expected_topics over the cross-cutting KB + tables over the overlays). Requires a writable entry group in your project.

run._doc: Runnable CASE: hybrid = --mode=doc WITH --dataset. Build the stackoverflow replica, then create KB entries + per-table overlays.
run.topic: Stack Overflow public dataset knowledge base

golden_facts: the dataset is a BigQuery mirror of the Stack Exchange Data Dump for Stack Overflow, the public Q&A site for programmers
Stack Overflow is modeled as many independent entity tables that join through integer ids (unlike a single denormalized events table)
posts, comments, votes, and badges reference users.id via owner_user_id or user_id; an answer references its question via posts_answers.parent_id -> posts_questions.id
the integer-id joins are the canonical analytical paths (e.g. top answerers, acceptance rate by tag, reputation vs activity)
the source Posts table (distinguished by PostTypeId) is split in BigQuery into posts_questions (PostTypeId=1) and posts_answers (PostTypeId=2)
one row per question; id is the primary key referenced by answers, votes, and comments
owner_user_id references users.id (the asker) and tags lists the question's topic labels that correspond to rows in the tags table
accepted_answer_id references posts_answers.id (the answer the asker accepted, null if none)
score is upvotes minus downvotes; answer_count and view_count are engagement counters
one row per account; id is referenced by posts (owner_user_id), comments, and badges (user_id)
reputation is the user's core earned trust score, driven by the votes their posts receive
up_votes and down_votes are the counts of votes the user has cast
